metrics.narrative: No quantified value case exists. On the 17 Apr 2026 discovery call the conversation was scoping-level: Brice (Zycus, is_rep:true) said his role is 'understanding the needs and the pain points of our prospects in order to make customized demo', and only 8 minutes were left to probe pain ('I'm going to use these 8 minutes to ask questions regarding what you have today, what are your pain points'). No cost, cycle-time, headcount, or savings figure was articulated by the buyer. Quantifying the procurement pain (current S2P process cost, ERP fragmentation impact) is a primary move at the 5 Aug 2026 discovery call.

champion.narrative: No confirmed champion; Myriam Alcaraz is a developing coach. On 17 Apr she was the host who scheduled the meeting (Anshu, Zycus: 'I would like to thank Myriam for being so kind always on the calls for scheduling this call') and took the internal-follow-up action items (sharing 20-30 documents with colleagues, checking budget with peers). That is genuine facilitation, which is what has developed; what is missing is evidence she will advocate for Zycus over iValua/SAP Ariba or carry us to the economic buyer. Bertrand Lagache is the named evaluator for the 5 Aug discovery but has no recorded advocacy yet. Convert Myriam from coordinator to champion and test her access to power.

competition.narrative: Confirmed competitive, not sole-source: Salesforce names iValua and SAP Ariba as competitors (source: SF Competitors__c), and the buyer's intent to 'Send the RFP to the 3 or 4 suppliers' (Avoma 17 Apr 2026) confirms a multi-vendor field. On the call Brice probed the current state - 'Is this still the case, or you were using Ariba, at least?' - suggesting Ariba may be part of the incumbent or evaluated stack, though this was not resolved. We cannot yet articulate the win wedge (Merlin agentic AI, integration depth) because the preferred vendor, the incumbent system, and Quadient's top criteria are still unknown. Establishing the competitive shortlist and what they value most is a priority at discovery.

identify_pain.narrative: Specific pain is not yet confirmed. The 17 Apr call was a Zycus introduction ('you're not aware about our solutions') and ran short on discovery, with only 8 minutes for pain probing. Myriam's stated intent was to 'understand your solution first' before sharing problems. Inferred pain (not yet confirmed by the buyer) is fragmented S2P tooling across multiple ERPs - Brice asked 'how many ERP do you have?' and which modules manage S2P today - but no pain owner has confirmed urgency or a compelling event. Surfacing and owning the pain is the central goal of the 5 Aug discovery.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are immature. An NDA is in play: the planned next step is to 'Review the material shared after NDA execution', implying an NDA has been or is being executed to enable document sharing (source: SF Next_Step__c). No MSA/SOW, legal review path, procurement channel, or InfoSec process has been discussed on the recorded call. With a CISO/DPO (Eric Bleicher) present at account level, expect an InfoSec review later; visibility will come once discovery defines scope. There is no contract-expiration forcing function on record.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is mapped. The two buyer-side contact roles are Bertrand Lagache (Manager Production Purchasing, Evaluator) and Myriam Alcaraz (Global Manager, Procurement & Legal Operations, Influencer) (source: SF OpportunityContactRole) - both operational, neither a budget owner. On the 17 Apr call Myriam signalled she must check internally on budget: she 'will follow up to discuss the integration of the new tool ... and gather detailed information from peers in other departments regarding their projects and available budget', confirming budget authority sits elsewhere and is unknown. Account-level CFO/COO contacts exist in Salesforce but none is proven on this opp. Identifying and reaching the economic buyer is a ranked move.

decision_process.narrative: The process the buyer is running is partly visible from the call itself: this is an early evaluation where Quadient/Neopost intends to issue an RFP - an action item on 17 Apr was to 'Send the RFP to the 3 or 4 suppliers' (speaker_id 2, is_rep:true paraphrase of buyer intent), indicating a competitive multi-vendor process rather than sole-source. Myriam is acting as internal coordinator, gathering peer input and budget data before advancing. The formal approval chain (who signs, procurement gating) is not yet established. Mapping the buying committee and approval steps is explicitly part of the planned 5 Aug 2026 discovery agenda.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are only partially visible. One concrete, repeated requirement surfaced: integration with their retained AI contract-review tool - 'Myriam Alcaraz will follow up to discuss the integration of the new tool with the existing contract review AI tool' (source: Avoma 17 Apr 2026). Brice's discovery questions also flagged ERP landscape and module fit as evaluation axes ('how many ERP do you have? Which one', 'what module do you use today to manage your S2P processes?'). Beyond integration and module fit, the full weighted criteria set is undocumented; pin it down at the 5 Aug discovery.

flags: No discovery call completed despite 16 Jun AI roundtable attendance (14 days ago). Convert event interest into qualification meeting immediately. Ask: What procurement pain are you solving? What is broken today (current system/process)? Who owns the budget for source-to-pay transformation? Are other vendors being evaluated? What is the decision timeline and process? Without discovery, MEDDPICC remains entirely dark and positioning is blind.

AI positioning opportunity if RFP includes automation/AI requirement. Quadient's AI roundtable attendance signals interest in AI-driven procurement; if discovery confirms this, lead with Merlin (agentic sourcing, supplier negotiation automation, intelligent intake-to-contract) as the wedge vs legacy ERP-centric platforms (SAP Ariba) or suite players without agentic depth (Coupa, GEP). Make Merlin the Phase 1 or Phase 2 centerpiece to win on innovation where others offer workflow automation only.

Map the Economic Buyer on the 5 Aug discovery call. Ask Bertrand who controls the budget and signs the contract. Quadient account contacts include five CFOs (Dubromel, Lemonnier, Assous, Delpech, Placier) and two COOs (Labadie, Wozelka). Identify which one owns the procurement transformation budget and secure a path to engage them. Without EB access, commercial discussions and contract approval are blocked.
Champion gap: no Champion identified. Bertrand (Evaluator) has shown no advocacy behavior and has not been re-engaged since event.

Multi-vendor RFP confirmed (iValua, SAP Ariba), but competitive positioning is blind. No intelligence on incumbent, preferred vendor, or what Quadient values most (cost, AI, speed, integration ease). Use the discovery call to surface: who is incumbent (if any), what Quadient is scoring vendors on (must-have criteria), and their AI appetite — then position Merlin negotiation agent and rapid deployment as differentiation vs iValua's complexity and Ariba's cost/lock-in. Without this intelligence, RFP response will be generic and lose.

MEDDPICC darkness: all MEDDPICC elements (Metrics, Economic Buyer, Decision Criteria, Decision Process, Paper Process, Identify Pain, Champion) are gaps. Deal cannot progress to the relevant stakeholder or Shortlisted without these fundamentals.

Convert AI event interest to discovery call within 7 days. Bertrand Lagache attended the AI roundtable on 16 Jun 2026 (14 days ago) but no follow-up discovery has been scheduled or completed. Event-sourced leads cool rapidly when not acted on; without a discovery call this week, the opportunity will drift into dormancy. Book a discovery call with Bertrand and at least one finance or operations executive (CFO, CPO, COO) by 7 Jul 2026 (within 7 days) to qualify pain, metrics, and decision authority before interest fades.

Lead with Merlin AI positioning, then validate fit and surface pain. The AI roundtable event context signals interest in AI-driven procurement. On the discovery call, open with how Merlin (agentic negotiation, sourcing automation, spend intelligence) solves real procurement pain (manual sourcing bottlenecks, supplier negotiation inefficiency, invoice-processing cost), then ask Quadient to describe their current pain and quantify the impact. This positions Zycus as the AI-first platform while surfacing the business case and pain owner needed to build urgency and justify the investment.

Establish the competitive shape early: SAP Ariba is already embedded for marketing categories and iValua is logged; confirm at 5 Aug whether this is a multi-vendor RFP or sole-source and tailor the consolidation pitch accordingly.
best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage deal cooling in a confirmed competitive RFP; the win levers are restarting momentum before the 5 Aug discovery, mapping power, and converting the AI-roundtable interest into qualification.

competitive_position.summary: Multi-vendor RFP confirmed: iValua and SAP Ariba are named competitors per Salesforce; Quadient plans to 'send the RFP to 3 or 4 suppliers' per 17 April call. Threat levels are unrankable — no intelligence on incumbent, preferred vendor, or competitive positioning exists because discovery has not progressed beyond first-touch introduction. iValua and Ariba are both active in the RFP (status: active, threat_level: medium by default absent disqualifying evidence), but no buyer sentiment, pricing comparison, or differentiation discussion has occurred. Current positioning is blind: without pain, EB access, or criteria discovery, cannot articulate why Zycus wins. The wedge (Merlin AI negotiation agent, rapid deployment, deep ERP integration) has not been activated because the buyer's priorities are unknown.

deltas.subject: Multi-thread to power on the first discovery call. The deal is single-threaded at the operational level (Bertrand Lagache, Manager Production Purchasing, Evaluator role). deltas.subject: Lead with Merlin AI positioning, then validate fit and surface pain. The AI roundtable event context signals interest in AI-driven procurement.
